---
title: "Module 02: Regularization"
---

## Learning objectives

- Motivate **ridge** and **lasso** when predictors are correlated or \(p\) is large relative to \(n\).
- Contrast **continuous shrinkage** (penalized regression) with **discrete** subset search (AIC / stepwise).
- Recognize when **elastic net** is a reasonable default between the two extremes.

## Key messages

- Regularization trades a controlled amount of **bias** for lower **variance** and more stable predictions.
- On **synthetic genes**, we know which coefficients *should* matter — use that to sanity-check LASSO paths. On **penguins**, shrinkage is about real collinearity among bill / flipper / mass.

## Concept outline

### Ridge (\(L_2\))

- Shrinks coefficients smoothly; rarely forces exact zeros.
- Good default when many predictors move together and you do not need sparsity for interpretation.

### Lasso (\(L_1\))

- Can set coefficients to **exactly zero** — variable selection + prediction.
- When sparsity matches the generating process (gene notebook), paths are pedagogically clean.

### AIC and stepwise

- **Discrete** search over subsets; different failure modes than lasso (instability, greedy steps).
- Teaching beat: compare **backward vs lasso** coefficient panels from Monday materials.

### Elastic net (bridge)

- Mixing parameter \(\alpha \in [0,1]\): blend ridge-like stability with lasso-like zeros when you are unsure.

## Course links

- **Slides:** [Monday deck](../slides/day-01-monday.qmd) (ridge, lasso, AIC, elastic net slides)
- **Notebooks — genes:** [linear-regression-lasso.Rmd](../notebooks/linear-regression-lasso.Rmd) — uses **`glmnet`** for penalized regression and **`MASS::stepAIC`** in the code for stepwise AIC.  
- **Notebooks — penguins:** [penguins-body-mass.Rmd](../notebooks/penguins-body-mass.Rmd) — ridge/LASSO on **body mass** with the same `glmnet` idea on real, correlated measurements.
- **Curriculum:** regularization and model-selection beats under Monday in [curriculum outline](../curriculum-outline.qmd)
- **Module library:** [Back to all modules](index.qmd)

::: {.callout-note appearance="minimal"}
## Draft / TODO

- Short **side-by-side schematic** of coefficient paths (ridge vs lasso) as a static figure or link to slide export.
- Optional reading list on **post-selection inference** (one sentence + link for instructors).
:::
